items.q: What is a 'problématique' in the research project?
items.correct: A focused research question the project sets out to answer
items.misconception: A list of vocabulary to memorise
items.why: Students think the project is descriptive; a strong problématique poses an arguable question, not just a topic.

items.q: Why must sources be referenced in the IRP?
items.correct: To credit them and let the work be verified
items.misconception: Because longer bibliographies always score higher
items.why: Students under-reference; a clear bibliography shows the breadth and reliability of research and avoids plagiarism.

items.q: How is the research project assessed at A-level?
items.why: Students expect a written exam; the IRP is defended and discussed aloud in French.

items.q: In the Individual Research Project, what is a 'problématique'?
items.correct: A focused research question guiding the whole project
items.why: The answer examiners want is: A focused research question guiding the whole project. “A grammar exercise” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Why should a student use sources in French for the IRP?
items.correct: To research the topic authentically and develop language skills
items.misconception: Because English sources are banned in all schools
items.random_wrong: Because French sources are always shorter
items.why: Correct answer: To research the topic authentically and develop language skills. “Because English sources are banned in all schools” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What does showing 'esprit critique' in the project involve?
items.correct: Evaluating and comparing different viewpoints and sources
items.misconception: Copying one website word for word
items.why: Correct answer: Evaluating and comparing different viewpoints and sources. “Copying one website word for word” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Where is the Individual Research Project assessed?
items.correct: In the speaking exam, through an oral presentation and discussion
items.misconception: In a written grammar test only
items.why: The answer examiners want is: In the speaking exam, through an oral presentation and discussion. “In a written grammar test only” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
